sections.title: Abstract nouns – generic vs specific
sections.content:
Abstract uncountables (love, time, music, knowledge, history) take **no article** when used generally, but take **the** when made specific by context:

• ∅ **Love** is essential. (general)
• **The love** she has for her children is incredible. (specific)
• ∅ **History** repeats itself.
• **The history** of the Czech Republic is fascinating.
• ∅ **Music** helps me concentrate.
• **The music** in the café was too loud.

items.good: The love she has for her children is incredible.
The history of the Czech Republic is fascinating.

sections.title: The + adjective for groups
sections.content:
Use **the + adjective** to refer to a whole group of people:

• **the rich**, **the poor**, **the elderly**, **the homeless**, **the unemployed**
• **the French**, **the British**, **the Chinese**, **the Japanese** (nationalities ending in -ch / -sh / -ese)

*The rich should help the poor. The elderly deserve our respect.*

sections.title: Subjects of study and job titles
sections.content:
**Subjects of study** take no article: study ∅ **history**, ∅ **economics**, ∅ **mathematics**, ∅ **chemistry**.

**Title + name** takes no article: ∅ **Doctor Smith**, ∅ **Professor Novák**, ∅ **President Obama**.

**Unique role in context** takes the: **the** President, **the** Prime Minister, **the** Queen.

Compare: *She's **a** doctor* (profession, one of many) vs *∅ **Doctor Smith** is on holiday* (title + name) vs ***The** doctor will see you now* (specific one).

items.good: The doctor will see you now.

sections.title: Geographic names – the full picture
sections.content:
**No article** (∅): most countries (France, Japan), continents (Europe, Asia), cities (Prague, London), most streets (Oxford Street), individual peaks with 'Mount' (Mount Everest, Mount Snowdon), lakes with 'Lake' (Lake Geneva).

**With 'the'**: plural-form countries (**the** Netherlands, **the** Philippines), country-with-common-noun (**the** UK, **the** USA, **the** Czech Republic), regions (**the** Middle East, **the** Caribbean), mountain ranges (**the** Alps, **the** Himalayas), bare-name peaks (**the** Matterhorn), rivers (**the** Thames, **the** Vltava), seas and oceans (**the** Pacific, **the** Mediterranean), canals (**the** Suez Canal), deserts (**the** Sahara), a few cities (**the** Hague, **the** Bronx).

right.title: With 'the'
points: Country + noun: the UK, the Czech Republic

sections.title: Proper nouns and names
sections.content:
**With 'the'**: newspapers (**the** Times, **the** Guardian), most hotels (**the** Hilton, **the** Ritz), descriptive-name buildings (**the** White House, **the** Empire State Building), ships (**the** Titanic), letter-acronym organisations (**the** BBC, **the** EU, **the** UN), 'the University of X'.

**No article** (∅): company names (Microsoft, Google), palaces named after a person (Buckingham Palace, Windsor Castle), shops named after their founder (Harrods, Tesco), 'X University' (Cambridge University, Oxford University), word-acronym organisations (NATO, UNESCO, NASA).

left.title: With 'the'
points: the BBC, the EU, the UN
